r8_kernel_matrix.construction: Q_N[m,n] = phi((gamma_m - gamma_n)/W) ; N x N symmetric
r8_kernel_matrix.comment: Quartic bump (1-x^2)^2_{|x|<=1} is NOT a positive-definite function (its Fourier transform takes negative values), so this kernel matrix need not be PSD. Empirically lambda_min < 0 for W >= 2.

r24_gram_matrix.construction: Q = H H^T where H[i,j] = exp(-(c_i - gamma_j)^2/(2 sigma^2)) ; M x M
r24_gram_matrix.comment: Q = H H^T is a Gram matrix: x^T Q x = ||H^T x||^2 >= 0, so Q is positive-semidefinite by definition. Empirically lambda_min > 0 (matrix is even strictly positive-definite in our settings).

interpretation: The conflict in f9 is fully explained by matrix construction. r8's Q_N[m,n] = phi((gamma_m - gamma_n)/W) is PSD only if phi is a positive-definite function (Bochner: its Fourier transform must be nonnegative). A generic compactly supported 'bump' like (1-x^2)^2 fails this criterion and yields lambda_min < 0. r24's Gram construction Q = H H^T is PSD by definition regardless of the basis chosen.
